name: Cardiovascular & Longevity
description: Variants associated with coronary artery disease risk, thrombosis, and lifespan.

snps.description: Paired with rs7412 to determine APOE haplotype (e2/e3/e4). e4 raises Alzheimer's and CAD risk.

CT.note: Possible single e4 allele — interpret with rs7412.

CC.note: Possible e4/e4 — interpret with rs7412; elevated Alzheimer's + CAD risk.

snps.description: Paired with rs429358 to determine APOE haplotype.

CT.note: One e2 allele possible — often protective for lipids.

TT.note: e2/e2 possible — rare, associated with type III hyperlipoproteinemia.

snps.description: Best-characterized CAD-risk locus; risk allele ~25% population-attributable risk.

AG.note: One risk allele — modestly elevated CAD risk.

GG.note: Two risk alleles — elevated CAD risk; focus on LDL particle size + inflammation.

snps.description: Centenarian gene — G allele associated with longer lifespan across populations.
Strong predictor of elevated Lp(a); associated with CAD and aortic stenosis.

CT.note: Risk allele present — consider measuring Lp(a).

CC.note: Homozygous risk — strongly associated with elevated Lp(a).

snps.description: Classic inherited thrombophilia variant; venous clot risk rises further with estrogen, surgery, pregnancy, or immobility.

CC.note: No Factor V Leiden variant detected at this site.

CT.note: Factor V Leiden carrier — baseline venous clot risk is meaningfully elevated.

TT.note: Factor V Leiden homozygous — strongest common inherited clotting-risk pattern in this file type.

snps.description: Common inherited thrombophilia variant associated with higher prothrombin levels and venous thrombosis risk.

GG.note: No prothrombin G20210A variant detected at this site.

AG.note: Carrier — venous thrombosis risk is elevated versus baseline.

AA.note: Homozygous carrier — higher clotting-risk signal than heterozygous carriage.
